jokes.text: You are going to change America in a way that usually only war and fluoride can.
jokes.punchline: Comparing political impact to war and fluoride
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly unexpected comparison that feels both absurd and oddly accurate. Kent's bureaucratic precision makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Not in a Jewish way, either. In a, like, selected from a very short list of 10 way.
jokes.punchline: Awkward clarification that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Classic Dan overcompensating and making things worse. Awkward but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Some people say three microwaves is overkill. My response is always, 'Tapas.'
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical 'tapas' justification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan logic - completely confident in completely absurd reasoning. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: You know that saying, 'Anyone can run for president'? Joe Thornhill is the only reason that that's utter bullshit.
jokes.setup: Referencing the common saying about democracy
jokes.punchline: One person disproves the entire concept
jokes.explanation: Sharp political observation with good structure. Could apply to any incompetent candidate.

jokes.text: My nostrils feel like Vietnam.
jokes.punchline: Comparing nostrils to Vietnam war
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully excessive metaphor that's both unexpected and oddly apt. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Is this the beginning of the Rapture or what?
jokes.punchline: Comparing staff chaos to biblical apocalypse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but fairly predictable absurd comparison. Solid but not memorable.

jokes.text: Mississippi is chock-full of assholes. I don't trust the Chinese. I'm not gonna be able to pass a single piece of legislation that's really gonna make any fuck of a difference in your life.
jokes.setup: Imagining speaking his mind on campaign
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest platform escalating to nihilistic truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben cynicism with escalating honesty. Dark, subversive, and brutally realistic political commentary.

jokes.text: He's Amy without a conscience and he's Dan without the five percent that needs to be loved.
jokes.setup: Describing Ericsson as perfect campaign manager
jokes.punchline: Combining worst traits of existing characters
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly constructed character analysis that perfectly captures Amy's ruthlessness and Dan's neediness while creating a monster.

jokes.text: Not the whole bag. A specimen cup full of little Mikes.
jokes.setup: Gary asking if bag is full of his fluids
jokes.punchline: Overly specific clarification with cute nickname
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable overshare with mild wordplay. Mike's obliviousness sells it but it's not particularly clever.

jokes.text: All my 'troops' are in a cold bag now, okay? I just gotta get them to the doctor so he can begin the assault on 'Egg Mountain.'
jokes.punchline: Military metaphors for reproduction
jokes.explanation: Mike's inappropriate military metaphors for intimate medical procedures. Cringe-funny but memorable phrasing.

jokes.text: I'd rather cede control of the CIA to Pakistan than see Dan lead a campaign.
jokes.setup: Expressing how much he doesn't want Dan as campaign manager
jokes.punchline: Extreme geopolitical metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben hyperbole with political specificity. Escalates to absurd but believable extreme for the character.

jokes.text: I can't do the 'be nice to people' because that's what Dan's doing. Also, 'cause I can't do it.
jokes.punchline: Admitting fundamental personality flaw
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy self-awareness about her limitations. The second beat makes it funnier by revealing deeper truth.

jokes.text: Dan, watching you try to be nice is like watching a baby smoke a cigarette... it's kind of cool, but also very disturbing.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre but apt metaphor with twisted logic
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant metaphor that's both disturbing and weirdly accurate. Highly quotable and completely unexpected comparison.

jokes.text: That was quite a long journey for that joke. I admire your stamina.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on his own joke construction
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-humor about joke construction itself. Shows Ericsson's calculating nature even in casual conversation.

jokes.text: He'd make a good croupier... Never thought of that. He's very good at reading people, you know? Right, hitting on drunk divorcees, yeah. That's his level.
jokes.punchline: Escalating from compliment to brutal assessment
jokes.explanation: Good character assassination that feels organic to the conversation. Ericsson's manipulation is subtle but effective.

jokes.text: Oh, but you know, he's my asshole. You need a better asshole.
jokes.punchline: Endearing possessiveness using 'asshole' literally and figuratively
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's twisted affection for Gary. The wordplay works but isn't particularly sophisticated.

jokes.text: Someone around here needs to get a new job, and it ain't me.
jokes.setup: Doctor telling Gary he needs a new job
jokes.punchline: Gary's delusional confidence about his position
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary delusion. The irony works because we know his job security is actually terrible.

jokes.text: I should be the 'three-mile candidate.' 'Three Mile' has negative connotations. There was a nuclear disaster there. Yeah. Well, there might be one here too.
jokes.punchline: Accidentally invoking nuclear disaster, then doubling down
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from bad slogan to threatening nuclear disaster. Amy's correction makes Selina's response funnier.

jokes.text: Peeing in a bush, being talked to on a porch. It's kind of like being a dog.
jokes.punchline: Comparing country activities to dog behavior
jokes.explanation: Unexpected comparison that actually makes sense once you think about it. Good observational humor.

jokes.text: What in the living fuck?
jokes.setup: Mike claiming his job isn't a hobby
jokes.punchline: Explosive reaction to obvious statement
jokes.explanation: Selina's exasperation is funny but the setup is weak. The profanity does most of the work.

jokes.text: It's like a flowchart for people who don't know what firearms do.
jokes.setup: Seeing guns on walls next to animal heads
jokes.punchline: Reducing hunting trophies to educational aid
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about hunting trophy displays. Good visual joke that works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: Oh, well... please, by all means, 'Leonardo.'
jokes.setup: Jake claiming to be a Renaissance man
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic nickname upgrade
jokes.explanation: Decent sarcasm that deflates pretension. Selina's delivery would sell this better than the writing alone.

jokes.text: No, they were pre-killed.
jokes.setup: Selina asking if he killed the animals
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact response about animals being 'pre-killed'
jokes.explanation: Maddox's casual response to awkward question. 'Pre-killed' is nicely absurd euphemism.

jokes.text: He talks the talk, but all he did in Iraq was wait for the bombing to stop, then post Facebook photos of himself sitting on a gold-plated bidet.
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd image of social media vanity
jokes.explanation: Vivid, specific image that perfectly captures military posturing vs. actual service. The bidet detail makes it memorable.

jokes.text: You fucking idiot.
jokes.punchline: Maddox's immediate angry reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment. Shows Maddox's true nature beneath the politeness. Relies mainly on surprise.

jokes.text: I could take a dump on it, if you need a clincher.
jokes.setup: Mike spilling wine on Amy's rug
jokes.punchline: Offering to make damage worse in most disgusting way
jokes.explanation: Classic Ben escalation to absurd extreme. Character-specific but somewhat predictable grossout humor.

jokes.text: I also do that with my men.
jokes.setup: Compliment about identifying wine by smell
jokes.punchline: Applying wine expertise to sexual partners
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable overshare that's funny because it's so inappropriate for the setting. Amy's social awkwardness showing.

jokes.text: I've eaten hummus with a pen cap. Don't tell me how I can eat.
jokes.setup: Being told potatoes can't be eaten alone
jokes.punchline: Extreme example of eating anything with anything
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy desperation revealed through absurd eating habits. Specific, unexpected, and character-revealing.

jokes.text: 'S.S. Starch.'
jokes.punchline: Naval pun on starch content
jokes.explanation: Quick pun that builds on the setup. Decent wordplay but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Do you think that I'm ruthless enough? I think you have just the right amount of grit in your oyster.
jokes.punchline: Gary's mangled metaphor attempt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary - trying to be profound but getting the metaphor slightly wrong. Character-specific but not brilliantly written.

jokes.text: Can I be a complete fucking bitch? Please don't talk about yourself that way.
jokes.setup: Selina asking if she can be ruthless enough
jokes.punchline: Gary's sweet response missing the practical question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary sweetness completely missing the point. Shows his devotion while highlighting his political uselessness.

jokes.text: Mike's masturbating at work... I caught him coming out of the bathroom stall with a bag.
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of the discovery
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable revelation that works mainly through cringe factor. Gary's gossipy nature drives the humor.

jokes.text: Did you bring any extra dressing? I hear the next course is gonna be pulled pork... pork that has been pulled.
jokes.punchline: Sexual double entendre about masturbation
jokes.explanation: Ben can't resist making uncomfortable jokes. The double entendre is obvious but the escalation works.

jokes.text: So everybody knows that I was in there masturbating into a cup now?
jokes.punchline: Mortified directness about the situation
jokes.explanation: Mike's humiliation is funny but the line itself isn't particularly clever. Relies on cringe factor.

jokes.text: I have a sister who was... well, she didn't have the... it kind of all went wrong within the tubing...
jokes.setup: Trying to relate to Mike's IVF struggles
jokes.punchline: Spectacularly inappropriate oversharing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent moment - trying to be helpful but being completely inappropriate. The clinical language makes it worse.

jokes.text: Please do. Yay me. Yay me.
jokes.setup: Ben sarcastically supporting her for campaign manager
jokes.punchline: Pathetic self-celebration
jokes.explanation: Amy's defeated sarcasm about her own prospects. The repetition sells the desperation.

jokes.text: Oh, please do not tell me you are one of those fucking weirdos that celebrates three month-a-versaries.
jokes.punchline: Amy's assumption it's pathetic milestone celebrating
jokes.explanation: Good Amy cynicism about relationship milestones. Shows her contempt for sentimentality.

jokes.text: Oh, please do not tell me you are one of those fucking weirdos that celebrates three month-a-versaries. It's actually a year.
jokes.punchline: Amy's assumption being wrong makes her look worse
jokes.explanation: Amy's cynicism backfires when she's wrong about the timeline. Shows her relationship problems.

jokes.text: The spicy burrito juice.
jokes.setup: Continuing to complain about burrito attack
jokes.punchline: Overly specific description of condiment danger
jokes.explanation: Jonah's continued victimhood over minor incident. The specificity of 'spicy burrito juice' is funny.

jokes.text: People like me, people hire me, people date me, and people fuck me because I'm triple-A fuckin' awesome and no other reason.
jokes.setup: Defending his worth after Dan's uncle comment
jokes.punchline: Delusional self-assessment with crude confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah delusion. The 'triple-A' rating and crude language show his insecurity masked as confidence.

jokes.text: You know what? The only reason you have a fuckin' job is because your uncle is 'Donald Chump.'
jokes.punchline: Name-calling pun on Donald Trump
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that feels lazy. Dan's anger makes it work somewhat but the wordplay is uninspired.

jokes.text: Selina's gonna lose and President Maddox is gonna have you both executed!
jokes.punchline: Jonah's complete misunderstanding of how American politics works
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah ignorance - thinking presidents execute political opponents. Shows his complete civic illiteracy.

jokes.text: Wake up and smell the fuckin' burrito juice.
jokes.punchline: Mixing common phrase with earlier burrito reference
jokes.explanation: Good callback that transforms familiar phrase. The absurdity of burrito juice as wake-up smell works.

jokes.text: I'd rather be shot in the fucking face than serve as vice president again. Seriously, in the fucking face!
jokes.setup: Maddox suggesting she be his VP
jokes.punchline: Extreme violent preference over political demotion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina rage about being demoted. The repetition and specificity ('in the fucking face') sells the fury.

jokes.text: Desperate Dan, flailing Amy, crippled Gary, jizzy Mike.
jokes.punchline: Cruel but accurate nicknames for each
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly economical character assassination. Each nickname is perfect and cruel. Great rhythm and structure.

jokes.text: I'm gonna shoot Mike and Gary in the head, I'll poison Amy, I'll behead Dan.
jokes.punchline: Escalating to specific murder methods
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that shows Selina's fury. Different murder methods for different people adds detail.

jokes.text: That desperate dick covered in medals that he got on eBay, by the way?
jokes.punchline: Claiming his military decorations are fake
jokes.explanation: Good character assassination with modern twist. eBay detail is specific and believable enough to be funny.

jokes.text: You know what? You stop interrupting me, you stupid fucking interrupty guy.
jokes.punchline: Making up 'interrupty' as an insult
jokes.explanation: Selina's made-up word shows her frustration. 'Interrupty guy' is childish but effective insult.

jokes.text: I wouldn't spread that around, Gary.
jokes.setup: Gary saying he's a Prosecco man
jokes.punchline: Suggesting it's embarrassing information
jokes.explanation: Ben's casual masculinity policing. The understatement makes it funnier than direct mockery would be.

jokes.text: I carry around Selina's big, heavy bag of her tampon bullshit.
jokes.punchline: Crude but accurate description of his menial duties
jokes.explanation: Gary's resentment finally showing through drunk honesty. 'Tampon bullshit' captures his frustration perfectly.

jokes.text: Fuck you, Dan, you minor-league gigolo!
jokes.setup: Learning Dan got campaign manager job
jokes.punchline: Gary's drunken, oddly specific insult
jokes.explanation: Great Gary drunk insult. 'Minor-league gigolo' is perfectly specific and insulting for Dan.

jokes.text: When I was a kid... a bunch of older kids dared me to kill this stray dog. And I did.
jokes.punchline: Admitting to animal murder
jokes.explanation: Shocking reveal that perfectly fits Dan's character. Dark humor that recontextualizes everything about him.

jokes.text: I torch cars, you know... give me the nuclear codes.
jokes.punchline: Joking about nuclear capability
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from personal crime to potential global destruction. Shows Selina's dangerous ambition.

jokes.text: We should throw your expired cum at Jonah's door.
jokes.punchline: Weaponizing reproductive waste for revenge
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd revenge plan that connects multiple plot threads. Disgusting but perfectly in character for Ben.
